text.title: Verbatim To Backlog Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Verbatim To Backlog Pattern. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Apply the four-step transform: extract → cluster → frame → prioritise. Skipping any step breaks evidence linkage.
rule.rationale: Apply the four-step transform: extract → cluster → frame → prioritise. Skipping any step breaks evidence linkage.

rule.statement: Every backlog card carries ≥2 verbatim quotes with source + timestamp.
rule.rationale: Every backlog card carries ≥2 verbatim quotes with source + timestamp.

rule.statement: Cards are framed as Job Stories: 'When <trigger>, I want <motivation>, so I can <outcome>'.
rule.rationale: Cards are framed as Job Stories: 'When <trigger>, I want <motivation>, so I can <outcome>'.

rule.statement: Cards are prioritised by RICE: Reach × Impact × Confidence ÷ Effort.
rule.rationale: Cards are prioritised by RICE: Reach × Impact × Confidence ÷ Effort.

rule.statement: Quotes are preserved verbatim; no PM rewrites ('improve onboarding') in card titles.
rule.rationale: Quotes are preserved verbatim; no PM rewrites ('improve onboarding') in card titles.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-verbatim-to-backlog-pattern.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
